metadata.notes: All values extracted from game data files, not wiki estimates. Curve X values represent input parameters (health %, caps count, mutation count, etc.) and Y values represent the effect magnitude.

Aristocrat's.effect: Damage and Energy Resistance scale with caps
Aristocrat's.interpretation: X = caps held, Y = DR/ER multiplier. At 0 caps: 0% bonus. At 20,000 caps: 1% bonus. At 40,000 caps: 10% bonus. Logarithmic scaling - severely diminishing returns.
Aristocrat's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to 20% DR/ER'. Game data shows max multiplier of 10 at 40,000 caps. This is likely a multiplier applied to base armor value, not a flat percentage. The actual effect depends on how the game applies this curve.
Aristocrat's.notes: Logarithmic curve. Nearly worthless below 20k caps.

Bolstering.effect: Damage Resistance increases as health decreases
Bolstering.interpretation: X = health percentage (0.0=dead, 1.0=full). Y = damage multiplier (lower = more DR). At 0% HP, 1 piece = 0.9x (10% DR), 5 pieces = 0.59x (41% DR). Kicks in below 75% HP. Description curve shows displayed value: up to +10 DR/ER per piece at 0% HP.
Bolstering.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +35 DR/ER at low health'. Game data shows multiplicative stacking: each piece multiplies incoming damage by 0.9 at lowest HP, for 5 pieces = 0.59x total (41% reduction). The desc curve shows up to +10 displayed per piece.

Chameleon.wiki_comparison: Wiki accurate - no numerical curve, it's a boolean effect.

Cloaking.effect: Stealth field when hit in melee

Auto-Stim.effect: Auto-use Stimpak when health drops below 25%

Ghoul Slayer's.effect: Reduces damage from ghouls by 15% per piece
Ghoul Slayer's.interpretation: Multiplicative stacking. Each piece multiplies ghoul damage by 0.85. 5 pieces = 0.85^5 = 0.4437 (55.6% reduction).
Ghoul Slayer's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '-15% damage from ghouls'. Game confirms 15% per piece with multiplicative stacking.

Hunter's.effect: Reduces damage from animals by 15% per piece
Hunter's.interpretation: Identical curve to all type-specific damage reduction effects. 15% multiplicative per piece.

Troubleshooter's.effect: Reduces damage from robots by 15% per piece

Zealot's.effect: Reduces damage from Scorched by 15% per piece

Assassin's.effect: Reduces damage from players by 15% per piece
Assassin's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '-8% damage from players' but game data shows 15% per piece multiplicative, same as all other type-specific reductions. MAJOR DISCREPANCY - wiki may reflect a PvP-specific nerf not in the curve data.

Exterminator's.effect: Reduces damage from bugs by 15% per piece

Mutant Slayer's.effect: Reduces damage from Super Mutants by 15% per piece

Life Saving.effect: 50% chance to auto-revive with Stimpak when downed

Regenerating.effect: Slowly regenerate HP when not in combat
Regenerating.interpretation: Flat 0.5 HP/s regeneration rate per piece. Not level-scaled.
Regenerating.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'slowly regenerate HP'. Game data: 0.5 HP/s per piece.

Unyielding.effect: Up to +3 to all SPECIAL stats (except END) when low health
Unyielding.interpretation: X = health percentage. Stepped function: +3 SPECIAL below 20% HP, +2 at 20-40%, +1 at 40-60%, +0 above 60%. Per piece, so 5 pieces = +15 to all SPECIAL (except END) at <20% HP.
Unyielding.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+3 to all stats except END when low health'. Game data CONFIRMS +3 per piece, with thresholds at 20%/40%/60% HP. 5 pieces = +15 total. Wiki is accurate.

Vanguard's.effect: Damage Resistance increases with higher health
Vanguard's.interpretation: X = current HP value (not percentage). Most benefit at full health. At very high HP (1000), 1 piece = 0.94x (6% DR), 5 pieces = 0.734x (26.6% DR). Sharp dropoff around 633-861 HP range. Description shows up to +6 displayed per piece.
Vanguard's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +35 DR/ER at high health'. Game data shows multiplicative per-piece stacking. Max benefit lower than Bolstering's max. 5pc Vanguard = 26.6% DR vs 5pc Bolstering = 41% DR.
Vanguard's.build_rating: A-TIER for full health builds, but mathematically weaker than Bolstering at extremes

Nocturnal.effect: DR/ER increases at night, decreases during day
Nocturnal.wiki_comparison: No dedicated curve file found; effect likely shares weapon_damagenight logic.

Mutant's.interpretation: X = number of mutations. Diminishing returns after 10 mutations. 1 piece at 10 mutations = 0.96x (4% DR). 5 pieces at 10 mutations = 0.815x (18.5% DR). Caps around 12+ mutations. Description shows up to 5% displayed per piece at 16 mutations.
Mutant's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+10 DR/ER per mutation up to 5'. Game data shows much more granular scaling with diminishing returns past 10 mutations.

Overeater's.effect: Damage reduction increases with higher food/water
Overeater's.interpretation: Main curve: X = food/water satisfaction (0-8 scale, where 8 = fully stuffed). At max (8.0): 40% damage reduction per piece. Zero benefit below 1.6 satisfaction. Ramps sharply above 4.0. Effect curve similar but X=0-40 scale.
Overeater's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '6% damage reduction when well-fed'. Game data shows up to 40 at max but this may be divided by armor pieces or have a cap. SIGNIFICANT DISCREPANCY - likely 40 is the raw curve value that gets processed further.
Overeater's.build_rating: S-TIER for full health tank builds when well-fed

Heavyweight.effect: Damage Resistance scales with carried weight
Heavyweight.interpretation: X = carry weight ratio (current/max, 0-1.5 where >1.0 = overencumbered). More DR when carrying more. 1 piece at max (1.5): 0.9x (10% DR). 5 pieces at max: 0.59x (41% DR). Description shows up to +10 displayed.

Weightless.interpretation: X = piece count. Weight multiplier: 1pc = 0.9x (10% lighter), 5pc = 0.59x (41% lighter). Description: up to 10% displayed.

Lucid.effect: Damage Resistance scales with rads taken
Lucid.interpretation: X = rads level (0-8 scale). More DR with more rads. 1pc at 8 rads: 0.94x (6% DR). 5pc at 8 rads: 0.734x (26.6% DR). Description shows up to 6% displayed.

Bolstering (Inverse Health DR).effect: DR increases as health decreases (flat DR version)
Bolstering (Inverse Health DR).interpretation: X = HP percentage. Flat DR value, not multiplier. +35 DR/ER at 0-10% HP, linearly drops, +0 above 61% HP. This is the RAW Bolstering backing curve for resistances.
Bolstering (Inverse Health DR).wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+35 DR/ER at low health'. Game data CONFIRMS +35 per piece max. Cuts off sharply above 60% HP.

Resistances Per Kill.interpretation: X = kill count (1-10). Y = DR bonus. +10 DR per kill, max +100 DR at 10 kills. Linear scaling.

Resistances via Caps (Aristocrat's backi.interpretation: X = caps. Y = flat DR/ER per piece. Linear: ~0.67 DR per 1000 caps. Max +20.1 DR at 30,000 caps.

Vanguard's (Proportional Health).effect: DR scales proportionally with current health
Vanguard's (Proportional Health).interpretation: X = HP percentage. Y = flat DR. Ramps from 0 at <50% to +35 DR at 90% HP. Linear within range.

Damage Resist via Caps.effect: Direct damage resistance scaling with caps (separate from Aristocrat's)
Damage Resist via Caps.interpretation: X = caps held. Y = flat DR. Max +100 DR at 29,000 caps. Non-linear: fast early, then accelerates again.

Resistances via Carry Weight.interpretation: X = current carry weight. No benefit below 200. +1 DR at 200, +20 DR at 350. Only activates when carrying heavy loads.

Powered (AP Regen).wiki_comparison: No curve file; AP regen handled separately.

Weapon Weight Reduction.interpretation: Flat 20% weapon weight reduction per piece. Does NOT stack multiplicatively - additive.
Weapon Weight Reduction.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '20% weapon weight reduction'. Game data CONFIRMS 20%.

Food/Drink/Chem Weight Reduction.effect: Food, drink, and chem weights reduced by 20%

Cavalier's.interpretation: X = piece count (0-5). Multiplicative stacking. Each piece = 0.9x damage taken while sprinting. 5 pieces = ~41% reduction.
Cavalier's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '75% chance to reduce damage by 15% while sprinting'. Game data shows DETERMINISTIC 10% per piece multiplicative, NOT probabilistic. MAJOR DISCREPANCY.

Sentinel's.interpretation: X = piece count (0-5). NOT perfectly multiplicative - slightly rounded. Each piece ~5% reduction. 5 pieces = 23% reduction.
Sentinel's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '75% chance to reduce damage by 15% while standing still'. Game data shows DETERMINISTIC ~5% per piece, NOT probabilistic. 5pc = 23%. MAJOR DISCREPANCY - much weaker than wiki claims.
Sentinel's.notes: Sentinel is SIGNIFICANTLY weaker than Cavalier in game data. Cavalier = 10% per piece, Sentinel = 5% per piece.

Less Damage Sprinting.effect: Reduces damage while sprinting (different from Cavalier)
Less Damage Sprinting.interpretation: X appears to be a cumulative value (0-25). Multiplicative 5% increments.

Less Damage Standing Still.effect: Reduces damage while standing still (backing for Sentinel)
Less Damage Standing Still.interpretation: Identical curve to lessdamagesprinting. 5% multiplicative per 5-unit increment.

Less Damage Blocking.interpretation: X = block value (0-75 in increments of 15). Each 15 units = 0.85x multiplier. Max 55.6% at 75. Multiplicative stacking.

Less Fall Damage.interpretation: Flat -50 to fall damage calculation per piece. Negative value = damage reduction.

Burning (Damage Attacker).interpretation: X = piece count. Slightly accelerating: ~19 fire damage per piece to melee attackers. 5 pieces = 96 fire damage.

Electrified (Damage Attacker).interpretation: X = piece count. ~18 energy damage per piece to melee attackers.

Frozen (Damage Attacker).interpretation: X = piece count. 12 cryo damage per piece. Weakest of the damage attacker effects.

Toxic (Damage Attacker).interpretation: X = piece count. ~12 poison damage per piece.

Aegis.effect: Incoming damage reduction scales per piece count
Aegis.interpretation: X = piece count (1-5). Y = incoming damage modifier. Linear 20 per piece. At 5 pieces = 100 (meaning 100% of incoming damage is... modified? This is likely a BASE value fed into another formula, not raw % reduction).
Aegis.notes: 4-STAR EXCLUSIVE. Power Armor only variant found.

Tanky.interpretation: X = piece count. Y = incoming damage modifier value. 200 per piece, up to 1000. 10x the Aegis values. These are large raw values likely used in damage formula, NOT direct % reduction.

Stalwart's.interpretation: X = piece count. Y = condition buff value. +5 per piece, max +25.

Rejuvenator's.effect: Regeneration scaling per team member count
Rejuvenator's.interpretation: 5 tiers, X = piece/team member count (0-5). Tier 1: 1 regen per piece. Tier 5: 6 regen per piece (max 30 at 5 pieces).

Sane.interpretation: X = stack count (0-8). Ramps linearly to 4 at 4 stacks, then slows. Max 6 at 8 stacks.

Other 4-Star Armor.notes: These 4-star effects exist as OMOD records but do not have dedicated curve files in the legendarymods/ directory. Their numerical effects are defined inline in the OMOD properties.

Low Health Increases Stats (PA).notes: Unyielding was DISABLED for Power Armor. This is why PA can't roll Unyielding.

Damage via Carry Weight (weapon).notes: Weapon damage scaling with carry weight disabled

Various 4-star disabled.notes: All zzz_ prefixed effects are disabled/removed from the legendary pool.

DEL (Deleted) 4-star.notes: DEL_ prefixed effects were WIP (Work In Progress) for 4-star system and deleted before release.

Anti-Armor.effect: Ignores a percentage of target's armor
Anti-Armor.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '50% armor penetration'. No curve file, flat value in OMOD.

Aristocrat's.interpretation: X = caps held. Y = damage bonus %. Max +50% damage at 29,000 caps. Non-linear scaling.
Aristocrat's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +50% damage at 29,000 caps'. Game data CONFIRMS 50% max. Wiki accurate.

Berserker's.interpretation: X = player's DR. Y = damage bonus %. Max +50% at 0 DR. Drops to +5% at 60 DR. Requires being nearly naked for full benefit.
Berserker's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +50% at 0 DR'. Game data CONFIRMS 50%. Wiki accurate.

Bloodied.interpretation: X = HP percentage (0.05-1.0). Y = damage bonus %. LINEAR from +130% at 5% HP to +0% at full HP. At 20% HP (bloodied threshold): ~104% bonus. At 50% HP: ~65% bonus.
Bloodied.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +80% at low health' but some sources say +95%. Game data shows MAX +130% at 5% HP. SIGNIFICANT DISCREPANCY - actual value much higher than wiki claims.
Bloodied.build_rating: S-TIER - highest potential damage bonus of any 1-star effect

Executioner's.effect: 50% more damage when target is below 40% health
Executioner's.interpretation: Flat 50% damage bonus. Always 50 regardless of level.
Executioner's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+50% damage to targets below 40% health'. Game data CONFIRMS 50%.

Furious.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+5% damage per consecutive hit'. No curve file - likely flat stacking in OMOD.

Gourmand's.effect: Damage bonus based on food/water satisfaction
Gourmand's.interpretation: X = food/water satisfaction (0-8). Y = damage bonus %. Linear 5% per point. Max +40% at full (8).
Gourmand's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +24% damage when well fed'. Game data shows up to +40%. DISCREPANCY - game value higher than wiki.

Instigating.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'double damage to full health targets'. Confirmed.

Junkie's.interpretation: X = addiction count (0-10). Y = damage bonus %. Linear +10% per addiction. Max +100% at 10 addictions.
Junkie's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+10% per addiction, max +50% at 5'. Game data shows it CONTINUES to +100% at 10 addictions. MAJOR DISCREPANCY if game has >5 addictions possible.

Mutant's.interpretation: X = mutation count (0-10). Y = damage bonus %. Linear +5% per mutation. Max +50% at 10 mutations.
Mutant's.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+5% per mutation up to +25% at 5'. Game data shows it CONTINUES scaling to +50% at 10 mutations. DISCREPANCY - wiki may be outdated.

Nocturnal.interpretation: X = game hour (0-24). Y = damage bonus %. +50% from 9pm-5am, +0% from 6am-8pm. Binary switch, no gradual transition.
Nocturnal.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+50% at night, -50% during day'. Game data shows +50% at night, +0% during day. NO PENALTY during day. DISCREPANCY.

Two Shot.effect: Fires additional projectile at reduced damage
Two Shot.interpretation: Second projectile does 20% of base damage. Total damage = 120% but accuracy reduced.
Two Shot.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'additional projectile'. Game data shows second shot = 0.2x (20%) of base damage. Effectively +20% damage with accuracy penalty.
Two Shot.notes: The 0.2 multiplier applies to the SECOND projectile only.

Damage via Health.interpretation: X = max HP. Y = damage bonus %. Linear: +0.1% per HP. At 300 HP: +30%. At 500 HP: +50%. Max +100% at 1000 HP.
Damage via Health.notes: Extremely powerful if HP stacking is possible.

Assassin's.wiki_comparison: Wiki sometimes lists lower PvP values, but curve shows flat 50%. PvP damage scaling may reduce this separately.

Stalker's (Accuracy Not In Combat).effect: Increased accuracy when not in combat

Damage Per Kill.interpretation: X = kills (0-10). Y = damage bonus %. +10% per kill, max +100% at 10 kills. Linear.

Cryptid Hunter's.notes: All creature-type damage bonuses are identical: flat 50%.

Explosive.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '20% of base damage as explosive'. Confirmed.
Explosive.notes: Legacy shotgun version exists as separate OMOD.

Faster Fire Rate.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+25% fire rate'. Game data CONFIRMS 25%.

Critical Damage.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+50% critical damage'. Standard value.

Faster Swing Speed (Melee).interpretation: Flat +40% swing speed. The gold standard melee 2nd star.
Faster Swing Speed (Melee).wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+40% swing speed'. Game data CONFIRMS 40%.

Last Shot.effect: Last round in magazine does bonus damage

Reduced Weight.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '90% reduced weight'. Game data CONFIRMS 90%.

Charged (Melee).interpretation: X = charge level (1-3). Y = damage multiplier. Tier 1: 0.5x, Tier 2: 1.5x, Tier 3: 3.0x. Massive scaling with charge.

Electricians.effect: Adds energy damage scaling with base damage
Electricians.interpretation: X = base weapon damage. Y = added energy damage. Scales from 11 to 25 energy damage based on weapon base damage. Low base weapons get proportionally more.

Fracturers.interpretation: X = base damage. Y = explosion damage added. 22-50 explosion damage. Better than Electricians for raw damage.

Voltaic (Energy + EMP).interpretation: Energy curve identical to Electricians. EMP curve: X = stack count (1-5). Y = EMP damage. 40 per stack, max 120.

Cold Shoulder (Cryo).interpretation: X = base damage. Y = cryo damage added. Highest base of all elemental 4-star effects: 44-91 cryo damage.

Sane (Weapon).interpretation: X = sanity stacks (0-8). Y = damage bonus %. Linear 5% per stack, max +40%.

Reload/Melee Speed Per Kill.effect: Reload or swing speed increases per kill
Reload/Melee Speed Per Kill.interpretation: X = kill count (1-10). Y = speed multiplier bonus. +3% per kill, max +30% at 10 kills.

scrip_values.notes: All categories have identical scrip values. 4-star gives 6 scrip.

sell_values.notes: PA has highest sell values. Melee and ranged weapons equal.
